briefing.headline: Rig up a Lipless Crankbait — steady spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Lipless Crankbait is a strong choice for spawn with clear water and stable conditions.

KVD: 'The bait HAS to hit something — rocks, grass, bottom. Simply retrieving in a straight line won't yield results. If the bait isn't hitting anything, stop reeling and let it sink until it does. Deflection and disturbance trigger strikes.'

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: 'The bait HAS to hit something — rocks, grass, bottom. Simply retrieving in a straight line won't yield results. If the bait isn't hitting anything, stop reeling and let it sink until it does. De
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: 'On shallow flats, use your rod tip and line size (10-20lb) to control depth. I want the bait hitting bottom. Every time it ticks cover, pop it and let it flutter — that's when fish bite.'
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: On clear water grass lakes, most anglers fish the inside line where fish spawn. But fish also spawn in the deeper scattered grass patches (7ft+) in the middle of big flats. Nobody else fishes it — that's a competitive advantage. A lot of larg...

Johnston: You have to be able to judge the size of bedding fish without catching them to make sure you're going to get the right ones. Spend time gathering waypoints on spawning areas before tournament day.
Robertson: During prespawn at Chickamauga, fish an offshore pattern with a lipless crankbait in grass, then go headhunting with a big swimbait for kicker bites.

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable spawn conditions have fish committed to beds in 1-5ft. Focus on hard-bottom areas in protected coves, pockets, and flats. Polarized glasses are essential for sight-fishing.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
